data.requirement: Aggregate crop productivity metrics (Yield and Production) by district and season to understand macro-level trends.
data.reason: Analyzing aggregated data helps identify overall productivity variations across districts and seasonal differences.

data.requirement: Include the crop type as a dimension to filter and segregate data.
data.reason: Crop type is a critical entity for analyzing specific productivity metrics and determining trends related to individual crops.

data.requirement: Normalize Yield and Production values based on the Area to calculate per-hectare productivity.
data.reason: This derived metric allows for fair comparison across districts with different cultivation areas and improves decision-making.

data.requirement: Integrate data abstraction levels to provide both high-level overviews and micro-level details for district-level productivity analysis.
data.reason: Macro-level summaries can highlight overall patterns, while micro-level exploratory data enables deep-dive analysis into specific districts and crops.

data.requirement: Calculate year-over-year yield trends for each crop type aggregated by district to support forecasting.
data.reason: Historical comparison helps identify productivity changes over time and supports predictive modeling.

data.requirement: Highlight anomalies in productivity metrics, such as districts with unusually high or low yield or production relative to area.
data.reason: Identifying anomalies can guide decisions to investigate and address the underlying causes.

visualization.requirement: Use a bar chart to compare aggregated productivity metrics (Yield and Production) by district and season.
visualization.reason: Bar charts are effective for comparing quantities across categorical dimensions like district and season.

visualization.requirement: Use a box plot to visualize the distribution of per-hectare Yield values across districts and by season.
visualization.reason: Box plots reveal productivity variability and can highlight outliers effectively.

visualization.requirement: Use a line chart to display yield trends over time for different crop types aggregated by district.
visualization.reason: Line charts are ideal for analyzing productivity changes and trends over a sequential timeline.

visualization.requirement: Use heat maps for correlation analysis between area, yield, and production by crop type and district.
visualization.reason: Heat maps are effective for visualizing relationships and intensity of values across multiple dimensions simultaneously.

visualization.requirement: Use a scatter plot to explore relationships between area and yield for specific crops and districts.
visualization.reason: Scatter plots allow clear visualization of correlation between continuous variables like area and yield.

visualization.requirement: Combine bar and line charts to represent crop seasonality and predict future yield trends visually.
visualization.reason: Combining chart types enhances readability of both periodic data and future projections in the same view.

interaction.requirement: Allow users to filter productivity data by district, season, and crop type.
interaction.reason: Filtering enables focused analysis based on specific geographic and agricultural parameters.

interaction.requirement: Enable brushing and linking across visualizations to connect data on districts selected in one view to related metrics in other views.
interaction.reason: Brushing and linking improve coherence and allow users to see interconnected insights efficiently.

interaction.requirement: Enable drill-down interaction to move from aggregated district-level productivity data to micro-level metrics like per-hectare yield.
interaction.reason: Drill-down empowers users to explore deeper insights within specific regions or crops.

interaction.requirement: Allow trend line generation with adjustable parameters (e.g., years to forecast) on line charts for yield trends.
interaction.reason: Interactive trend lines help users visualize and test different scenarios for yield prediction.

interaction.requirement: Enable sorting of categorical axes (e.g., district or crop type) based on metrics like average yield or total production.
interaction.reason: Dynamic sorting enhances the usability of the VAS by allowing users to focus on important entities first.

interaction.requirement: Support hover interactions on scatter plots and bar charts to show precise quantitative values and metadata for specific data points.
interaction.reason: Hover interactions provide immediate access to details without cluttering the visualization.
